page.title: Ellipsis Substitution And Reference
page.subtitle_html: Ellipsis Substitution And Reference - C2 cohesion through so, not, do so, one/ones, such, former/latter, and this/that reference.
page.subtitle_text: C2 cohesion through so, not, do so, one/ones, such, former/latter, and this/that reference.

blocks.body: {"level":"C2","intro":"Ellipsis, substitution and reference: compact C2 anchors for avoiding repetition while keeping reference clear.","rules":[{"label":"So and not","text":"Use so or not to replace a whole previous idea when the reference is unambiguous.","example":"The plan may work, and I hope so."},{"label":"Do so and one/ones","text":"Use do so for repeated actions, and one/ones for repeated countable nouns.","example":"They promised to revise the draft and did so promptly."},{"label":"Former, latter and reference","text":"Use the former, the latter, this, and that to connect ideas across sentences or clauses.","example":"The former is cheaper; the latter is more reliable."}]}

blocks.heading: C2 focus
blocks.body: This compact page anchors the English Sentence Builder C2 lesson on cohesive substitution and reference.
